steps.title: Recognize that factored form displays zeros as constants
steps.content: The question asks which form displays the zeros as constants. In factored form $(x-a)(x-b)$, the zeros $a$ and $b$ appear explicitly as constants in the expression. Other equivalent forms either require solving (such as vertex form) or don't reveal the zeros directly (such as expanded or partially factored forms).

steps.title: Identify and verify the factored form
steps.content:
Scanning the choices, only choice B is in fully factored form: $(x-2)(x-40)$. The zeros 2 and 40 are displayed directly as constants.

Verify by expanding:
$$(x-2)(x-40) = x^2 - 40x - 2x + 80 = x^2 - 42x + 80$$
This matches the original function. That matches choice B — B is correct.
